title: Novartis — Overview
html:
Novartis Account Overview

Governing Thought

Novartis is ZoomRx's broadest account by therapeutic diversity — spanning oncology (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Scemblix), hematology (PNH/ITP/wAIHA, Promacta), cardiovascular (Leqvio, Pelacarsen), immunology/dermatology (Remibrutinib/Rhapsido, Cosentyx), neuromuscular (Abelacimab), and gene therapy (Zolgensma) — across a partnership that dates back to 2019. Unlike J&J's breadth across project types or Pfizer's depth within vaccines, Novartis's complexity stems from the sheer number of therapeutic areas served, each with distinct competitive landscapes and buyer teams. HCP-Patient Conversations is the signature methodology at Novartis — it is the project type that opens and sustains engagement across nearly every brand. The Rhapsido (remibrutinib) PET, now at Wave 5, represents the deepest ongoing tracking engagement and the account's most mature recurring revenue stream. The SAGAN Answers demo signals that Novartis is exploring AI-augmented analytics — ZoomRx data feeding into Novartis internal tools — which is both an opportunity (integration = stickiness) and a threat (commoditization of raw data).

The Novartis Landscape: What You Need to Know

Novartis at a Glance

Dimension Detail
HQ Basel, Switzerland
CEO Vas Narasimhan, MD (since 2018)
Corporate transformation Post-Sandoz spinoff (Oct 2023) — pure innovative medicines company
FY 2025 Revenue ~$53B [UNVERIFIED — directional only]
Therapeutic Areas Cardiovascular, Oncology, Immunology, Neuroscience, Hematology
Brands with ZoomRx history 12+ (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Scemblix, Rhapsido/Remibrutinib, Fabhalta/Iptacopan, Leqvio, Cosentyx, Promacta, Abelacimab, Zolgensma, and others)
Partnership tenure Since 2019 (~7 years)
Dominant project type HCP-Patient Conversations
Active projects 4 (PSP Study Proposal, SAGAN Answers demo, Procurement file sharing, Abelacimab HCP-Pt)
Completed projects 20+ across multiple TAs

Why it matters: No other ZoomRx account touches this many therapeutic areas with such diverse competitive dynamics. Novartis is not a single-franchise account — it is a portfolio play where each TA operates as a semi-independent buying center. Expect overlapping but distinct stakeholder sets across oncology, hematology, immunology, and cardiovascular.

Portfolio by Therapeutic Area

Oncology — Kisqali, Pluvicto, Scemblix

Kisqali (ribociclib) — Breast Cancer

  • What it is: CDK4/6 inhibitor for HR+/HER2- breast cancer; Novartis's largest oncology brand
  • Revenue: ~$4B+ annually [UNVERIFIED — directional]
  • Market position: Competing with Ibrance (Pfizer) and Verzenio (Lilly) in a three-way CDK4/6 race; NATALEE trial (adjuvant data) differentiates Kisqali as the only CDK4/6 with OS benefit and adjuvant indication
  • ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations study (completed, 2024)
  • Competitive dynamic: Verzenio gaining share on monarchE adjuvant data; Kisqali's NATALEE data is the counter-narrative. Ibrance is the legacy incumbent with declining share.

Pluvicto (lutetium-177 vipivotide tetraxetan) — Prostate Cancer

  • What it is: Radioligand therapy (RLT) for PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC)
  • Revenue: ~$1.5B annually [UNVERIFIED — directional]
  • Market position: First-in-class radioligand therapy; manufacturing constraints limiting supply; SPLASH trial exploring earlier lines
  • ZoomRx work: PET study (completed, 2025), HCP-Pt Conversations (completed, 2024 and 2025)
  • Competitive dynamic: Unique MOA with no direct radioligand competitor. Competition is from other late-line mCRPC agents (enzalutamide, abiraterone, olaparib). MSL scientific education is critical given the novel modality.

Scemblix (asciminib) — CML

  • What it is: First-in-class STAMP inhibitor for chronic myeloid leukemia (CML)
  • ZoomRx work: Zero-cost PET (completed, 2023), HCP-Pt Conversations (completed, 2023)
  • Competitive dynamic: Competes with Tasigna (Novartis's own legacy TKI, also studied by ZoomRx), Sprycel (dasatinib), Bosulif (bosutinib), Iclusig (ponatinib). The zero-cost PET suggests the brand team was testing ZoomRx's PET capabilities — potential for a paid PET engagement.

Hematology — Fabhalta, Promacta, ITP/wAIHA/PNH

Fabhalta (iptacopan) — PNH

  • What it is: First oral complement factor B inhibitor for paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH)
  • ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations (completed, 2024)
  • Pipeline expansion: Label expansion into C3 glomerulopathy (C3G) and IgA nephropathy (IgAN)
  • Competitive dynamic: Competes with Soliris/Ultomiris (Alexion/AstraZeneca) and other complement inhibitors. Oral administration is the key differentiator.

Promacta (eltrombopag) — ITP

  • What it is: Thrombopoietin receptor agonist for immune thrombocytopenia (ITP)
  • ZoomRx work: Early-era Novartis project (pre-2023)

ITP/wAIHA/PNH HCP-Pt Study

  • What it was: Multi-indication hematology HCP-Pt study covering ITP, warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia, and PNH
  • ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations (completed, 2023)
  • Why it matters: Demonstrated ZoomRx's ability to run multi-indication studies within a single TA — a model that could be replicated in oncology (pan-tumor) or cardiovascular (multi-brand)

Cardiovascular — Entresto, Leqvio, Pelacarsen

Entresto (sacubitril/valsartan) — Heart Failure

  • What it is: Novartis's largest brand (~$7.3B annually [UNVERIFIED]); ARNI for heart failure (HFrEF and HFpEF)
  • ZoomRx work: None — zero ZoomRx engagement on a >$7B brand
  • Competitive dynamic: Facing generic/biosimilar risk timeline; PARADIGM-HF data well established. This is the single largest untapped brand at Novartis.

Leqvio (inclisiran) — Cholesterol/CV

  • What it is: siRNA therapy targeting PCSK9 for LDL-C reduction; administered twice yearly
  • ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations (completed, 2023)
  • Competitive dynamic: Competes with Repatha (Amgen) and Praluent (Sanofi/Regeneron). The twice-yearly injection model creates a unique patient conversation dynamic.

Pelacarsen — Cardiovascular (Pipeline)

  • What it is: Antisense oligonucleotide targeting Lp(a) — a genetically validated CV risk factor
  • ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations (completed, 2023)
  • Pipeline status: Late-stage; potential first-in-class for Lp(a) lowering
  • Why it matters: If approved, Pelacarsen opens an entirely new therapeutic category. ZoomRx's early HCP-Pt work positions the account for launch research.

Immunology/Dermatology — Remibrutinib/Rhapsido, Cosentyx

Remibrutinib (Rhapsido) — Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria (CSU)

  • What it is: Highly selective covalent BTK inhibitor for CSU; Phase 3 complete, trade name Rhapsido
  • ZoomRx work: PET tracking (Wave 5 as of Apr 2026 — the deepest recurring engagement), HCP-Pt Conversations (completed, 2025), Digital Tracking (completed, 2024)
  • Competitive dynamic: Competing with Xolair (omalizumab, Genentech/Novartis) in CSU. Dual allergist/dermatologist prescriber base creates distinct messaging requirements.

Cosentyx (secukinumab) — Psoriasis/PsA/AS

  • What it is: IL-17A inhibitor; ~$4.5B annually [UNVERIFIED]; biosimilar risk from 2027+
  • ZoomRx work: None — no active engagement
  • Competitive dynamic: Facing biosimilar erosion and competition from Skyrizi (AbbVie), Bimzelx (UCB), Taltz (Lilly). Defensive tracking needs will grow as biosimilar entry approaches.

Neuroscience — Kesimpta

Kesimpta (ofatumumab) — Multiple Sclerosis

  • What it is: Anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody for relapsing MS; self-administered subcutaneous injection
  • ZoomRx work: None — no active engagement
  • Competitive dynamic: Competes with Ocrevus (Roche/Genentech), Tysabri (Biogen), Briumvi (TG Therapeutics). Self-administration is the key differentiator.

Gene Therapy — Zolgensma

Zolgensma (onasemnogene abeparvovec) — SMA

  • What it is: One-time gene therapy for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)
  • ZoomRx work: Caregiver Qual + HCP Qual (completed, 2023)
  • Why it matters: Demonstrated ZoomRx's qual capabilities in a rare disease/gene therapy context. The caregiver perspective study was a novel approach.

Anticoagulation — Abelacimab (Pipeline)

Abelacimab — Anticoagulation

  • What it is: Anti-Factor XI monoclonal antibody; novel anticoagulation approach decoupling antithrombotic effect from bleeding risk
  • ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations + Market Intelligence (active, 2025-2026)
  • Pipeline status: Phase 3 trials ongoing
  • Why it matters: Active project representing ZoomRx's most recent new-brand entry at Novartis. If approved, could be a major launch with substantial tracking needs.

Pipeline to Watch

These assets will generate new project requests as they advance. Flag them in account planning conversations.

Asset Mechanism Stage Why It Matters
Abelacimab Anti-Factor XI (anticoagulation) Phase 3 Active ZoomRx project; launch research pipeline if approved
Remibrutinib (Rhapsido) BTK inhibitor (CSU) Phase 3 complete / launch PET at Wave 5; deepest tracking engagement — expect continued investment
Pelacarsen Lp(a) antisense (CV) Late-stage First-in-class for Lp(a); ZoomRx has early HCP-Pt baseline — positioned for launch tracking
Iptacopan (Fabhalta) expansion Complement factor B (C3G, IgAN) Phase 3 Label expansions could generate new HCP-Pt and ATU studies per indication
Pluvicto earlier lines (SPLASH) Radioligand therapy Phase 3 Moving to earlier prostate cancer = larger addressable market = more tracking
Cosentyx biosimilar defense IL-17A 2027+ biosimilar entry Defensive tracking needs will emerge as patent cliff approaches

ZoomRx's Service Footprint

The matrix below shows the breadth of ZoomRx engagement across Novartis's therapeutic areas. The HCP-Patient Conversations dominance is clear — this is ZoomRx's signature methodology at this account.

Project Type Oncology (Kisqali/Pluvicto/Scemblix) Hematology (PNH/ITP/wAIHA) CV (Leqvio/Pelacarsen) Immunology (Remibrutinib/CSU) Gene Therapy (Zolgensma) Anticoag (Abelacimab)
HCP-Patient Conversations X (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Scemblix) X (PNH, ITP/wAIHA/PNH) X (Leqvio, Pelacarsen) X (Remibrutinib) X
PET X (Remibrutinib Wave 5)
Digital Tracking X (Remibrutinib)
Qual (HCP/Caregiver) X (Zolgensma)
Market Intelligence X

Key takeaway: HCP-Patient Conversations is the universal entry point — 9 of 12 brand engagements used it. PET is active only on Remibrutinib (but at deep Wave 5 maturity). The massive gaps are: no PET on any oncology brand, no MSL/MEE on any brand, no ATU on any brand, and zero engagement on three of Novartis's biggest commercial brands (Entresto, Cosentyx, Kesimpta). The service footprint is wide but shallow — many brands touched once via HCP-Pt, few with recurring multi-service relationships.

Competitive Landscape Summary

Understanding competitive dynamics is essential because they drive the analytical narratives in every deliverable.

Brand Primary Competitor Competitive Dynamic Implication for ZoomRx Work
Kisqali Verzenio (abemaciclib, Lilly); Ibrance (palbociclib, Pfizer) Three-way CDK4/6 race in breast cancer; Kisqali differentiates on OS and adjuvant (NATALEE); Verzenio gaining on monarchE HCP-Pt revealed how oncologists discuss CDK4/6 choice with patients; messaging around OS benefit is the central brand narrative
Pluvicto Other late-line mCRPC agents (enzalutamide, abiraterone); no direct radioligand competitor First-in-class RLT; competitive battle is access/supply/PSMA testing rather than efficacy HCP-Pt and PET studies tracked how the novel modality is communicated; MSL scientific education is critical
Scemblix Tasigna (Novartis), Sprycel (BMS), Bosulif (Pfizer), Iclusig (Takeda) STAMP inhibitor differentiates from standard TKIs on mechanism; intra-Novartis cannibalization with Tasigna Zero-cost PET was a proving ground; Novartis has both the incumbent (Tasigna) and the disruptor (Scemblix)
Fabhalta Soliris/Ultomiris (AstraZeneca), Empaveli (Apellis) Oral vs. IV/SubQ complement inhibitors; oral convenience is Fabhalta's selling point HCP-Pt captured how the oral convenience message lands with patients accustomed to infusion therapy
Rhapsido (Remibrutinib) Xolair (omalizumab, Genentech/Novartis) BTK inhibitor vs. anti-IgE in CSU; dual allergist/derm prescriber base PET Wave 5 is the richest tracking data set on the account; competitive messaging tracked across specialist types
Entresto Generics/biosimilar timeline; Farxiga (AZ), Jardiance (Lilly/BI) expanding into HF Dominant ARNI but facing patent cliff; SGLT2 inhibitors encroaching on HF management No ZoomRx work — this is the single largest untapped brand on the account
Leqvio Repatha (Amgen), Praluent (Sanofi/Regeneron) siRNA twice-yearly vs. monthly PCSK9 mAbs; adoption limited by access and dosing logistics HCP-Pt captured the unique patient conversation around twice-yearly dosing model
Cosentyx Skyrizi (AbbVie), Bimzelx (UCB), Taltz (Lilly); biosimilar entry 2027+ Biosimilar erosion approaching; class competition from next-gen IL-17 and IL-23 agents No ZoomRx work — defensive tracking needs will emerge pre-biosimilar
Kesimpta Ocrevus (Roche), Briumvi (TG Therapeutics), Tysabri (Biogen) Self-administered subQ anti-CD20 vs. IV infusion competitors; convenience is the differentiator No ZoomRx work — growing MS brand with differentiated administration model

Bottom line: The Remibrutinib/Rhapsido vs. Xolair dynamic is where ZoomRx has the deepest data (Wave 5 PET). The Kisqali vs. Verzenio CDK4/6 race and the Pluvicto radioligand therapy story are the most analytically complex competitive narratives. Everything else is either early-launch (Fabhalta, Abelacimab) or completely unengaged (Entresto, Cosentyx, Kesimpta) — representing massive untapped opportunity.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

ZoomRx work: None — no active engagement
Competitive dynamic: Competes with Ocrevus (Roche/Genentech), Tysabri (Biogen), Briumvi (TG Therapeutics). Self-administration is the key differentiator.

What it is: One-time gene therapy for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)
ZoomRx work: Caregiver Qual + HCP Qual (completed, 2023)
Why it matters: Demonstrated ZoomRx's qual capabilities in a rare disease/gene therapy context. The caregiver perspective study was a novel approach.

Anticoagulation — Abelacimab (Pipeline)
Abelacimab — Anticoagulation

What it is: Anti-Factor XI monoclonal antibody; novel anticoagulation approach decoupling antithrombotic effect from bleeding risk
ZoomRx work: HCP-Pt Conversations + Market Intelligence (active, 2025-2026)

Why it matters: Active project representing ZoomRx's most recent new-brand entry at Novartis. If approved, could be a major launch with substantial tracking needs.
